name: Voltage Multistage 10
description: Four-channel staged modulator. Each slope runs as envelope, looping LFO or step sequencer with 10 shared stages, per-stage level/time/curve, quantiser, analog address and triggerable stage inputs.

controls.name: Slope
controls.description: Selects which of the four slopes is active for editing.

controls.name: Type
controls.description: Switches the selected slope between envelope, loop and step modes.

controls.name: Start / End
controls.description: Sets the active stage range for the selected slope.

controls.name: Quantiser
controls.description: Cycles the selected slope through internal scales.
Changes the output range of the selected slope.

controls.name: Stage Level / Time / Curve
controls.description: Each stage has a level slider, time knob and curve control.

controls.name: Stage Mode Toggle
controls.description: Selects on, off or slide behavior per stage.

controls.name: Start / Stop / Advance / Strobe / Enable / Sustain
controls.description: Panel controls for running, stepping and latching the sequencer core.

controls.name: Analog Address
controls.description: Chooses a stage by control voltage when strobe is latched.

inputs.name: Trigger Input 1-4
inputs.description: Trigger or gate inputs for the four slopes; each input normalises onward until broken.

inputs.name: Stage Select Inputs 1-10
inputs.description: Per-stage gate inputs that can jump directly to specific stages.

inputs.name: CV In
inputs.description: General CV input for matrix-style stage control.

inputs.name: Analog Address
inputs.description: Analog address input for latched stage selection.

inputs.name: Start / Stop
inputs.description: Starts or stops the selected slope.

inputs.name: Enable
inputs.description: Allows the clocked slope to continue only when high.

inputs.name: Sustain
inputs.description: Holds the active stage while high.

inputs.name: Strobe
inputs.description: Jumps to the stage selected by Analog Address.

outputs.name: CV A
outputs.description: Upper-stage CV output for the selected slope.

outputs.name: CV B
outputs.description: Lower-stage CV output for the selected slope.

outputs.name: Gate Out
outputs.description: Master gate output for the selected slope.

outputs.name: Ref Output
outputs.description: Falling ramp reference output tied to the selected stage length.

outputs.name: Stage Gate Outputs
outputs.description: Gate outputs for the 10 shared stages.

behaviors.name: Three slope modes
behaviors.description: Env outputs a triggered multistage envelope, Loop free-runs as an LFO and Step advances through voltages one stage at a time.

behaviors.name: Stage overlap and offsets
behaviors.description: Each slope can use a different slice of the 10 stages, or overlap with another slope to share stage controls.

behaviors.name: Latched analog address
behaviors.trigger: hold Strobe and move Analog Address
behaviors.description: When strobe is latched, an incoming CV can pick the current stage. A changing address can generate a new note stream without the clock staying high.

behaviors.name: Per-stage gate logic
behaviors.trigger: patch stage gate outs into Enable or Sustain
behaviors.description: Stage gate outputs can turn the sequencer into a self-gated envelope, a hold stage, or a loop that only advances on chosen steps.

behaviors.name: CV row split
behaviors.trigger: use CV A and CV B together
behaviors.description: CV A can glide through stage ranges while CV B gives the immediate stage voltage, which makes slide and time modulation easy to separate.

patchIdeas.name: Three-stage envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Slope 1 in Env mode with stages 1-3 active; Stage 1 sets attack, Stage 2 holds sustain and Stage 3 returns to zero. CV A drives a VCA and Gate Out retriggers the voice cleanly.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Voltage Multistage 10:
* Slope: 1
* Type: Env
* Start: Stage 1
* End: Stage 3
* Stage 2: sustain

VCA:
VCO:
Envelope:

- Keyboard (Gate) g> Voltage Multistage 10 (Trigger Input 1)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (CV A) >> VCA (CV)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Gate Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Looping LFO
patchIdeas.patch: Slope 2 in Loop mode with a short stage range and mixed curve settings becomes a custom LFO. CV B can move the loop length while Ref Output acts as a synced ramp for filter movement.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Voltage Multistage 10:
* Slope: 2
* Type: Loop
* Start: Stage 1
* End: Stage 4

LFO:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Voltage Multistage 10 (Trigger Input 2)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (CV A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Ref Output) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Step melody with slides
patchIdeas.patch: Slope 3 in Step mode with selected stages set to slide and others set to on. Stage 1-10 voltages become note rows, while a quantizer after CV A locks the sequence to a scale.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Voltage Multistage 10:
* Slope: 3
* Type: Step
* Quantiser: on

Quantizer:
VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Voltage Multistage 10 (Trigger Input 3)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (CV A) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Gate Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Analog address jump table
patchIdeas.patch: Strobe latched; Analog Address is driven by a slow random CV so the selected stage changes in a controlled but non-grid way. Gate Out and Ref Output still behave as stage-change clocks, so the patch stays rhythmically legible.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Voltage Multistage 10:
* Strobe: latched
* Start: Stage 1
* End: Stage 10

Random CV:
Clock:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) c> Voltage Multistage 10 (Strobe)
- Random CV (Out) >> Voltage Multistage 10 (Analog Address)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (CV B) >> Mixer (In 1)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Gate Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Ref Output) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Self-gated loop
patchIdeas.patch: Stage gate outputs feed Enable and Sustain, so one active stage can hold the slope until a later stage releases it. This turns the 10-stage editor into a pseudo-sequenced envelope with internal dependency.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Voltage Multistage 10:
* Slope: 4
* Type: Env

Envelope:
VCO:
VCA:
Filter:

- Clock (Out) c> Voltage Multistage 10 (Trigger Input 4)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Stage Gate Outputs) g> Voltage Multistage 10 (Enable)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Stage Gate Outputs) g> Voltage Multistage 10 (Sustain)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (CV A) >> VCA (CV)
- Voltage Multistage 10 (Ref Output) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
